Our Roofing Services in Greater Houston 
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we offer an extensive range of services to cater to residential and commercial needs. Each service is tailored to meet Greater Houston’s unique weather challenges and provide long-term value.
Residential Roofing Services in Greater Houston
Your home deserves a roof that combines durability with curb appeal. Whether you’re upgrading an aging roof, fixing leaks, or installing a brand-new system, we have the expertise to deliver exceptional results. From shingles to metal roofing, our team ensures your home is both beautiful and protected. Explore residential roofing services in Greater Houston.
Commercial Roofing Services in Greater Houston
Emergency Roofing Services for Greater Houston Properties
Storm Damage Repair Services
Greater Houston residents are no strangers to hail, hurricanes, and strong winds. These conditions can wreak havoc on roofs, causing leaks, missing shingles, and structural damage. Our storm damage repair services are designed to restore your roof quickly and effectively. See our storm damage repair services in Greater Houston.
Professional Roof Inspections and Maintenance
Regular roof inspections and maintenance are essential for extending the life of your roof. Our experts conduct thorough inspections, identify potential issues, and recommend cost-effective solutions to keep your roof in excellent condition. Schedule a roof inspection in Greater Houston.
Insurance Claims Assistance
Greater Houston’s Unique Roofing Challenges 
Greater Houston’s weather brings unique challenges that require specialized roofing solutions:
Summer Heat
Prolonged exposure to high temperatures can cause shingles to crack, curl, or warp, reducing their lifespan.
Torrential Rainfalls
Heavy rains often lead to leaks and water pooling, especially on flat or poorly maintained roofs.
Severe Storms
Hail, hurricanes, and high winds are common in Greater Houston and can cause significant damage.
High Humidity
The city’s humidity fosters mold growth, which can weaken roofing materials over time.
By choosing Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, you’re ensuring your roof is built to handle these challenges with ease.
Contact Us for the Best Roofing Services in Greater Houston 
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we prioritize quality, reliability, and customer satisfaction. Whether you need minor repairs, routine maintenance, or a full roof replacement, we have the expertise to meet your needs.
Frequently Asked Questions

Durable & Energy-Efficient Metal Roofing
We Protect Your Greatest Investment
CertainTeed 
Select Shingle Master
Flexible Financing
Options
24/7 Emergency
Services
Metal roofing in Texas is a fundamentally different investment than metal roofing in other parts of the country. Texas’s climate — intense summer heat, high UV exposure, Gulf Coast humidity in Houston, and severe hail and storm activity across DFW and Central Texas — demands a roofing system that performs under conditions most other markets never see. Asphalt shingles degrade faster under Texas conditions. Metal roofing doesn’t.
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we install standing seam metal roofing, exposed fastener metal panels, and metal shingles for residential and commercial properties throughout Texas including Houston, DFW, Austin, and College Station.
Why Texas Homeowners Choose Metal Roofing
The economics of metal roofing make more sense in Texas than almost anywhere else in the country. Here’s why:
A standard architectural shingle roof in Texas lasts 20 to 25 years under normal conditions — but Texas conditions aren’t normal. Intense UV exposure, extreme thermal cycling, and frequent severe weather events accelerate shingle wear across all Texas markets. Houston homeowners deal with Gulf Coast humidity and hurricane season. DFW homeowners face some of the most severe hail activity in the country — North Texas is part of what insurance professionals call Hail Alley. Austin and Central Texas homeowners face intense heat and increasingly severe storm seasons.
A properly installed metal roof lasts 40 to 70 years. Over that period, a Texas homeowner with a shingle roof will likely replace it two to three times. A metal roof installed once and maintained properly outlasts multiple shingle replacements — often at a lower total cost of ownership despite the higher upfront investment.
Metal Roofing and Texas Insurance Premiums
This is where metal roofing gets especially interesting for Texas homeowners. Many Texas insurance carriers offer premium discounts for impact-resistant roofing materials. A Class 4 impact-rated metal roof — the highest impact resistance rating available — can reduce your homeowner’s insurance premium by up to 30% annually. In a state where insurance rates have risen significantly due to storm claim frequency, particularly in Houston and DFW, that annual savings adds up fast and meaningfully reduces the payback period on your metal roof investment.
Metal Roofing and Energy Efficiency in Texas
Texas cooling seasons run eight to nine months of the year across most of the state. Traditional asphalt shingles absorb solar heat and transfer it into your attic, forcing your HVAC system to work harder. Metal roofing reflects solar radiation rather than absorbing it. Properly installed metal roofing with a reflective coating can reduce attic temperatures by 20 to 30 degrees and lower cooling costs by up to 25% during Texas summers.
Types of Metal Roofing Silver Ridge Installs
Standing seam metal roofing is our most popular metal system for Texas residential properties. The concealed fastener design eliminates exposed screws that can back out over time and create leak points — a critical advantage in high-rainfall markets like Houston. Standing seam panels expand and contract with temperature changes without compromising the seal, making them ideal for Texas’s extreme thermal cycling.
Exposed fastener metal panels are a cost-effective option for outbuildings, garages, and commercial structures where budget is the primary concern.
Metal shingles offer the aesthetic of traditional shingles with the durability of metal — a good option for homeowners in neighborhoods with HOA restrictions on roofing materials.
Free Metal Roofing Estimates Across Texas
Silver Ridge Roofing provides free metal roofing inspections and estimates throughout Texas including greater Houston, DFW, Austin, College Station, and surrounding communities. We’ll assess your current roof, explain your material options, and give you an honest comparison of metal versus shingle for your specific property and budget.
Call (713) 903-8011 or visit silverridgeroofing.com to schedule your free estimate today.
Get Your Free Quote
See what 
our customer 
is saying
Benefits of Metal Roofing 
Superior Weather Protection
We experience hurricanes, hailstorms, and heavy rain throughout the year. Metal roofing is built to withstand wind speeds up to 140 mph and is resistant to hail damage. Unlike asphalt shingles, metal roofing does not crack, curl, or deteriorate under extreme weather conditions.
Energy Efficiency & Cost Savings
One of the most significant benefits of metal roofing is its ability to reflect solar heat. This helps keep indoor temperatures cooler, reducing the strain on your air conditioning system and lowering energy bills. Many metal roofs come with reflective coatings that enhance this effect, making them an ideal choice for the Texas heat.
Longevity & Low Maintenance
A properly installed metal roof can last anywhere from 40 to 70 years, significantly outlasting traditional asphalt shingles. Additionally, metal roofs require minimal maintenance, needing only occasional inspections and cleanings to ensure they remain in top condition.
Fire & Mold Resistance
Unlike wood or asphalt, metal roofing is non-combustible, providing an extra layer of protection in case of wildfires or accidental fires. Additionally, metal roofs are resistant to mold, mildew, and algae growth, common problems in humid environments.
Environmentally Friendly Roofing Option
Metal roofing is one of the most sustainable roofing choices available. It is often made from recycled materials and can be fully recycled at the end of its lifespan. This makes it a responsible and eco-conscious investment.
Types of Metal Roofing We Offer 
Standing Seam Metal Roofing
This premium roofing style features interlocking panels with hidden fasteners, creating a sleek, modern look. It provides excellent protection against leaks and offers a clean, contemporary aesthetic.
Metal Shingles
For homeowners who prefer the traditional look of asphalt shingles but want the durability of metal, metal shingles are a great option. They mimic the appearance of wood, slate, or tile while providing enhanced longevity and weather resistance.
Corrugated Metal Roofing
A popular and cost-effective choice, corrugated metal roofing features ridges that improve structural integrity. It is lightweight, easy to install, and highly resistant to damage from storms and heavy winds.
Stone-Coated Steel Roofing
Stone-coated steel combines the strength of metal with the aesthetic appeal of traditional shingles or tile. It is ideal for homeowners looking for both durability and beauty.
Expert Metal Roof Installation 
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we follow a detailed and professional approach to metal roof installation. Our goal is to provide homeowners with a roofing solution that enhances both functionality and appearance.
Our Installation Process: 
We assess your current roof, discuss your needs, and recommend the best metal roofing solution.
Choose from various styles, colors, and finishes to match your home’s design.
Our trained professionals install your metal roof efficiently and securely, ensuring long-lasting performance.
We conduct a thorough quality check and leave your property spotless after installation.
Signs You Need a Metal Roof Replacement 
Your existing roof is showing signs of severe wear and tear.
There are noticeable leaks or rust patches on your metal roof.
Storm damage has compromised the structural integrity of your roof.
You’re experiencing increased energy costs due to poor insulation.
If you suspect it’s time for a metal roof replacement, our team is ready to help. Learn more about our roof replacement services.
Metal Roof Repair & Maintenance 
While metal roofing is known for its durability, occasional maintenance and repairs may be necessary, especially after severe weather conditions. Common metal roof repairs include:
Fixing loose or missing fasteners
Addressing minor dents caused by hail or debris
Repairing worn-out sealants or coatings
If you need metal roof repair, contact us for professional assistance. You can also explore our full range of roof repair services.
Choosing Between Metal Roofing and Asphalt Shingles 
For homeowners considering their roofing options, metal roofing offers distinct advantages over asphalt shingles. While asphalt is more affordable upfront, metal roofing provides long-term savings due to its durability, energy efficiency, and lower maintenance needs.
Explore More Roofing Options 
Not sure if metal roofing is the right fit? Browse our roof types guide for a full breakdown of available options.

Metal roofing in Texas is a fundamentally different investment than metal roofing in other parts of the country. Texas’s climate — intense summer heat, high UV exposure, Gulf Coast humidity in Houston, and severe hail and storm activity across DFW and Central Texas — demands a roofing system that performs under conditions most other markets never see. Asphalt shingles degrade faster under Texas conditions. Metal roofing doesn’t.

At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we install standing seam metal roofing, exposed fastener metal panels, and metal shingles for residential and commercial properties throughout Texas including Houston, DFW, Austin, and College Station.

**Why Texas Homeowners Choose Metal Roofing**

The economics of metal roofing make more sense in Texas than almost anywhere else in the country. Here’s why:

A standard architectural shingle roof in Texas lasts 20 to 25 years under normal conditions — but Texas conditions aren’t normal. Intense UV exposure, extreme thermal cycling, and frequent severe weather events accelerate shingle wear across all Texas markets. Houston homeowners deal with Gulf Coast humidity and hurricane season. DFW homeowners face some of the most severe hail activity in the country — North Texas is part of what insurance professionals call Hail Alley. Austin and Central Texas homeowners face intense heat and increasingly severe storm seasons.

A properly installed metal roof lasts 40 to 70 years. Over that period, a Texas homeowner with a shingle roof will likely replace it two to three times. A metal roof installed once and maintained properly outlasts multiple shingle replacements — often at a lower total cost of ownership despite the higher upfront investment.

**Metal Roofing and Texas Insurance Premiums**

This is where metal roofing gets especially interesting for Texas homeowners. Many Texas insurance carriers offer premium discounts for impact-resistant roofing materials. A Class 4 impact-rated metal roof — the highest impact resistance rating available — can reduce your homeowner’s insurance premium by up to 30% annually. In a state where insurance rates have risen significantly due to storm claim frequency, particularly in Houston and DFW, that annual savings adds up fast and meaningfully reduces the payback period on your metal roof investment.

**Metal Roofing and Energy Efficiency in Texas**

Texas cooling seasons run eight to nine months of the year across most of the state. Traditional asphalt shingles absorb solar heat and transfer it into your attic, forcing your HVAC system to work harder. Metal roofing reflects solar radiation rather than absorbing it. Properly installed metal roofing with a reflective coating can reduce attic temperatures by 20 to 30 degrees and lower cooling costs by up to 25% during Texas summers.

**Types of Metal Roofing Silver Ridge Installs**

Standing seam metal roofing is our most popular metal system for Texas residential properties. The concealed fastener design eliminates exposed screws that can back out over time and create leak points — a critical advantage in high-rainfall markets like Houston. Standing seam panels expand and contract with temperature changes without compromising the seal, making them ideal for Texas’s extreme thermal cycling.

Exposed fastener metal panels are a cost-effective option for outbuildings, garages, and commercial structures where budget is the primary concern.

Metal shingles offer the aesthetic of traditional shingles with the durability of metal — a good option for homeowners in neighborhoods with HOA restrictions on roofing materials.

**Free Metal Roofing Estimates Across Texas**

Silver Ridge Roofing provides free metal roofing inspections and estimates throughout Texas including greater Houston, DFW, Austin, College Station, and surrounding communities. We’ll assess your current roof, explain your material options, and give you an honest comparison of metal versus shingle for your specific property and budget.

When it comes to Flat Roofing, durability, weather resistance, and expert installation are key. Local climate presents unique challenges, from scorching summers and high humidity to heavy rainfall and occasional hurricanes. Without proper installation and maintenance, flat roofs can be prone to water pooling, leaks, and premature deterioration.
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we specialize in Flat Roofing, offering top-tier roofing solutions tailored to withstand the harsh weather. Whether you’re a homeowner or business owner, our expert roofing team provides high-quality materials and professional craftsmanship to ensure your roof remains strong and durable for years to come.
Get Your Free Quote

our customer 
is saying
Why Choose Flat Roofing ?
Flat roofs have become increasingly popular for both commercial and residential properties. The benefits of a flat roof go beyond aesthetics, here’s why they are a great choice:
Cost-Effective Solution
Flat roofs require fewer materials and less labor compared to traditional sloped roofs, making them a budget-friendly option.
Energy Efficiency
Flat roofs allow for energy-efficient materials like TPO roofing and reflective coatings that reduce heat absorption, lowering cooling costs.
Space Utilization
Many property owners use flat roofs for solar panels, rooftop patios, HVAC systems, or even green roofing solutions.
Easier Maintenance & Repairs
With a flat roof, inspections, maintenance, and repairs are safer and easier to perform, reducing long-term costs.
However, given unpredictable weather patterns, it’s essential to work with professional roofing contractors to ensure proper installation, high-quality materials, and ongoing maintenance.
Best Flat Roofing Materials 
Selecting the right flat roofing material is critical for long-lasting performance. Here are the best options based on unique weather conditions:
TPO Roofing 
Thermoplastic Polyolefin (TPO) is a lightweight, UV-resistant, and cost-effective material that helps combat extreme heat. It offers excellent waterproofing, making it a top choice for commercial and residential properties.
Learn more about TPO roofing.
Modified Bitumen Roofing 
This asphalt-based material is designed for durability, flexibility, and waterproofing. It withstands temperature fluctuations and provides excellent protection during storm season.
Built-Up Roofing (BUR)
Also known as tar and gravel roofs, BUR consists of multiple layers of bitumen and reinforcing fabrics. It’s ideal for heavy foot traffic and offers superior waterproofing.
Roof Coatings for Extended Lifespan
Applying roof coatings enhances your roof’s durability, preventing leaks and extending its lifespan by reflecting UV rays and reducing thermal expansion.
Weather Challenges for Flat Roofing 
Weather is unpredictable, and flat roofs are particularly susceptible to:
Heavy Rainfall & Drainage Issues
Flat roofs must have an efficient drainage system to prevent water pooling, which can lead to leaks and roof deterioration.
Extreme Heat & Sun Exposure
Hot summers and intense UV rays can degrade roofing materials over time. Reflective coatings and energy-efficient materials like TPO roofing help combat this issue.
Storm & Hurricane Risks
We are no stranger to strong winds, hail, and hurricanes. Without proper reinforcement, flat roofs can suffer significant damage. That’s why regular inspections and maintenance are essential.
Tip: Schedule routine inspections, especially before hurricane season, to ensure your roof is in optimal condition.
Flat Roof Installation & Repair
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we specialize in:
New Flat Roof Installation
Professional-grade installation for long-lasting, durable roofing solutions.
Flat Roof Repairs
Quick, efficient repairs to fix leaks, cracks, and storm damage.
Flat Roof Replacement
Upgrade your old roof with modern, high-performance materials.
Preventative Maintenance
Extend the lifespan of your roof with routine inspections and maintenance.
Need a flat roof installation or repair? Contact us today for a free estimate!
Industries & Properties We Serve
We provide Flat Roofing services for:
Commercial Buildings
Offices, warehouses, retail spaces.
Residential Homes
Flat roof designs for modern aesthetics and energy efficiency.
Industrial Facilities
We customize our roofing solutions to meet your specific needs.
Frequently Asked Questions 
TPO roofing is a great option because it’s energy-efficient, UV-resistant, and durable. Modified bitumen and BUR are also excellent choices for weather resistance.
With proper maintenance, a flat roof can last between 20–30 years. Using roof coatings can extend its lifespan.
Yes, humidity, storms, and heat make regular inspections essential. Flat roofs should be checked twice a year to prevent issues like leaks or drainage problems.
Yes! Flat roofs are ideal for installing solar panels, helping property owners reduce electricity costs and improving energy efficiency.
Proper drainage installation and routine maintenance are key. Low-slope roofing may be a better option if your roof has consistent water pooling issues.

![](https://silverridgeroofing.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Built-Up-4.webp)

### Built-Up Roofing (BUR)

![](https://silverridgeroofing.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Coatings-8.webp)

![](https://silverridgeroofing.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Hurricane-4.webp)

### Storm & Hurricane Risks

We are no stranger to strong winds, hail, and hurricanes. Without **proper reinforcement**, flat roofs can suffer significant damage. That’s why regular inspections and maintenance are essential.

**Tip:** **Schedule routine inspections, especially before hurricane season, to ensure your roof is in optimal condition.**

Extend the lifespan of your roof with **routine inspections and maintenance**.

## Industries & Properties We Serve

We provide **Flat Roofing** services for:

![](https://silverridgeroofing.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Commercial-12.webp)

### Commercial Buildings

Offices, warehouses, retail spaces.

![](https://silverridgeroofing.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Residential-11.webp)

### Residential Homes

Flat roof designs for **modern aesthetics and energy efficiency**.

![](https://silverridgeroofing.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Industry.webp)

### Industrial Facilities

We customize our roofing solutions to meet your specific needs.

## Frequently Asked Questions

TPO roofing is a great option because it’s **energy-efficient, UV-resistant, and durable**. Modified bitumen and BUR are also excellent choices for weather resistance.

With **proper maintenance**, a flat roof can last between **20–30 years**. Using [**roof coatings**](https://silverridgeroofing.com/roof-coatings-houton/) can extend its lifespan.

Yes, **humidity, storms, and heat** make regular inspections essential. Flat roofs should be checked **twice a year** to prevent issues like leaks or drainage problems.

Yes! Flat roofs are ideal for **installing solar panels**, helping property owners reduce electricity costs and improving energy efficiency.

Proper **drainage installation** and **routine maintenance** are key. Low-slope roofing may be a better option if your roof has consistent water pooling issues.

Roll roofing in Texas serves a specific and practical purpose — it’s the right solution for low-slope structures like garages, sheds, porches, and outbuildings where a full shingle or membrane system isn’t cost-justified. Across Texas markets from Houston to DFW to Austin, roll roofing is commonly used on accessory structures and low-slope additions where budget and simplicity matter more than longevity.
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we install mineral-surfaced roll roofing and modified bitumen systems on low-slope structures throughout Texas including Houston, DFW, Austin, and College Station.
When Roll Roofing Makes Sense in Texas
Roll roofing isn’t the right solution for every structure — and we’ll tell you honestly if it isn’t. Here’s when it makes sense:
Low-slope roofs with a pitch between 1:12 and 2:12 that don’t justify the cost of a full TPO or shingle system. Accessory structures like detached garages, workshops, and storage buildings where a 10 to 15 year lifespan is acceptable. Budget-constrained projects where getting a weathertight roof quickly and affordably is the primary goal. Rental properties and commercial outbuildings where minimizing upfront cost is more important than longevity.
If your structure needs a longer-lasting solution, Silver Ridge will tell you — a TPO or coating system often makes more sense for commercial low-slope applications in Texas’s high-rainfall and high-heat environment.
The Importance of Proper Installation in Texas
Roll roofing fails prematurely in Texas almost always for the same reason — improper installation. Houston’s annual rainfall of 50 inches, DFW’s severe storm activity, and Texas’s extreme thermal cycling all stress seams and laps in roll roofing systems. A poor installation that skips proper overlap distances, adhesive application, or edge sealing will fail within a few seasons in the Texas climate.
Silver Ridge installs roll roofing systems with correct seam overlaps, full adhesive coverage, and sealed edges at every penetration and perimeter. We pull permits where required and inspect the decking condition before installation begins.
Free Roll Roofing Estimates Across Texas
Silver Ridge Roofing provides free roll roofing estimates throughout Texas including greater Houston, DFW, Austin, College Station, and surrounding communities. Call (713) 903-8011 or visit silverridgeroofing.com to schedule your free estimate today.

Low slope roofing across Texas presents challenges that don’t exist in drier or cooler climates. Houston averages 50 inches of annual rainfall. DFW sees intense hail seasons. Austin and Central Texas face extreme thermal cycling from hot summers and occasional hard freezes. A low slope roofing system that performs in one Texas market needs to be specified correctly for another — one size doesn’t fit all across a state this large and climatically diverse.
At Silver Ridge Roofing & Construction, we install and repair low slope roofing systems for commercial and residential properties throughout Texas including Houston, DFW, Austin, and College Station.
What Is Low Slope Roofing?
Low slope roofing refers to any roof with a pitch between 1:12 and 3:12 — roofs that are nearly flat but have enough slope to drain water. This category includes most commercial buildings, flat-roof residential additions, detached garages, and industrial facilities. Low slope roofs require different materials and installation techniques than steep slope roofs — standard shingles are not designed for low slope applications and will fail prematurely if installed incorrectly.
Low Slope Roofing Systems Silver Ridge Installs
TPO (Thermoplastic Polyolefin) is the most common commercial low slope system in Texas markets. It’s heat-welded at the seams, highly reflective, and performs well in both Houston’s high-rainfall environment and the intense UV exposure across all Texas markets. TPO systems installed correctly can last 20 to 30 years.
Modified bitumen is a proven low slope system for both commercial and residential applications. It’s installed in multiple layers and provides excellent waterproofing performance in high-rainfall markets like Houston. Modified bitumen is often the right choice for low slope residential additions and smaller commercial structures.
Roof coatings — elastomeric, silicone, and acrylic — are applied over existing low slope systems that are structurally sound but showing surface wear. A coating extends the life of an existing system by 10 to 15 years at a fraction of the cost of full replacement. Silver Ridge performs coating assessments as part of every low slope inspection to determine whether a coating or full replacement is the right call for your specific roof.
The Number One Enemy of Low Slope Roofs in Texas
Ponding water — water that sits on a roof surface for more than 48 hours after rainfall — is the leading cause of premature low slope roof failure across all Texas markets. Every low slope installation Silver Ridge performs includes a drainage assessment. Even the best roofing membrane fails if water has nowhere to go. We evaluate drain placement, slope direction, and potential ponding areas before installation begins and address drainage issues as part of the scope of work.
How Long Does a Low Slope Roof Last in Texas?
A properly installed TPO system lasts 20 to 30 years. Modified bitumen lasts 15 to 20 years. Roll roofing on low slope structures lasts 10 to 15 years. Roof coatings extend existing system life by 10 to 15 years. The actual lifespan in Texas depends heavily on installation quality, drainage design, and maintenance — a poorly installed system in Houston’s rainfall environment will fail in 5 years regardless of material quality.
Free Low Slope Roofing Estimates Across Texas
Silver Ridge Roofing provides free low slope roofing inspections and estimates throughout Texas including greater Houston, DFW, Austin, College Station, and surrounding communities. Call (713) 903-8011 or visit silverridgeroofing.com to schedule your free inspection today.

## Why Choose Low Slope Roofing?

**Local climate presents unique challenges** for low slope roofs. ## Best Materials for Low Slope Roofing

Choosing the right roofing material is essential for performance and longevity. Here are some of the most reliable materials used for **low slope roofing**:

![](https://silverridgeroofing.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/tpo-8.webp)

TPO (Thermoplastic Polyolefin) is a highly durable and energy-efficient roofing material. It resists heat, UV rays, and punctures, making it a popular choice for businesses. [Learn more about TPO roofing.](https://silverridgeroofing.com/tpo-roofing-houton/)

Humidity levels create an environment for mold and mildew to develop on roofs. Regular maintenance and choosing moisture-resistant materials can help prevent these issues.
